title: Edit a task description

story.i_want_to: update the description of an existing task
story.so_that: I can fix typos or refine what a task means without deleting and re-adding it

rationale: Typos happen, and task scope evolves. Forcing users to delete and re-create a task just to fix wording is clunky and loses the original creation timestamp and status. An edit command keeps the workflow smooth.

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `todo edit 3 "Updated description"` changes the description of task id=3.
The task's id, status, created_at, and completed_at fields are not modified by edit. The updated_at field is set to the current ISO 8601 timestamp.
The app prints 'Updated task {id}: "{old_description}" -> "{new_description}"' to stdout, showing both old and new values.
If the task ID does not exist, the app prints 'Error: Task {id} not found.' to stderr and exits with code 1.
Running `todo edit 3` without a new description prints 'Error: Please provide a new description. Usage: todo edit <id> <description>' to stderr and exits with code 1.
Running `todo edit` without any arguments prints 'Error: Please provide a task ID and new description. Usage: todo edit <id> <description>' to stderr and exits with code 1.
If the new description is empty or contains only whitespace, the app prints 'Error: Description cannot be empty.' to stderr and exits with code 1 without modifying the task.
If the new description is identical to the current description, the app prints 'No changes made — description is already: {description}' to stdout and exits with code 0.
If the target task has status 'done', the app prints a warning 'Warning: Task {id} is already completed.' to stderr but still applies the edit.
If the provided ID is not a positive integer, the app prints 'Error: Task ID must be a positive number.' to stderr and exits with code 1.

test_cases.name: Edit updates description and preserves other fields
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1, description='Old text', status='pending', created_at='2026-03-25T10:00:00'
expected_results: Task updated_at is set to current timestamp

test_cases.name: Edit non-existent task returns error
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1
expected_results: Raises ValueError or returns error indicating task not found

test_cases.name: CLI edit command end-to-end
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1 'Buy groceries'
steps: Run `python todo.py edit 1 'Buy groceries and snacks'` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: stdout contains 'Updated task 1: "Buy groceries" -> "Buy groceries and snacks"'

test_cases.name: Empty description is rejected
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1 'Buy groceries'
expected_results: stderr contains 'Error: Description cannot be empty.'

test_cases.name: Same description shows no-change message
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1 'Buy groceries'
steps: Run `python todo.py edit 1 'Buy groceries'` with HOME=tmp_dir

test_cases.name: Multi-word description without quotes works
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1 'Buy groceries'
steps: Run `python todo.py edit 1 Buy groceries and snacks` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: Description updated to 'Buy groceries and snacks'

test_cases.name: Editing a completed task shows warning
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing task id=1, status='done'
steps: Run `python todo.py edit 1 'Updated text'` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: stderr contains 'Warning: Task 1 is already completed.'

test_cases.name: Non-numeric ID shows error
steps: Run `python todo.py edit abc 'New text'` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: stderr contains 'Error: Task ID must be a positive number.'

technical_notes: CLI args: sys.argv[2] is the ID, ' '.join(sys.argv[3:]) is the new description
This allows multi-word descriptions without requiring quotes in some shells
Find task by ID, update description field, set updated_at, save back to file
Strip whitespace from new description before validation
Add 'updated_at' field to data model (nullable, set on edit)
All error messages to stderr, all success messages to stdout
